topic: Sharp critical-completeness threshold for proximal identification of long-term ATE via outcome-bridge functions when the completeness condition on negative-control proxies approaches violation

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp critical completeness threshold for proximal ATE point identification when the outcome-bridge operator is weak, rank deficient, or has nonunique bridge solutions.

source_refs.claim: Negative-control point identification is framed through proxy rank/completeness conditions; when proxy information is insufficient, the paper turns to testing rather than a sharp failure-boundary characterization.
source_refs.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Semiparametric proximal inference depends on bridge equations and completeness, while also noting that bridge uniqueness is stronger than what some target-functionals require.
source_refs.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Shows that proximal counterfactual means can be identified without unique bridge functions, exposing target-functional identification as distinct from bridge identification.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Partial-identification bounds are proposed when completeness fails and bridge or causal-effect point identification is unavailable.
source_refs.author: AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Completeness-type identification hypotheses in nonparametric endogenous models can be empirically untestable in a strong sense, motivating threshold or sensitivity analysis rather than pretesting.
source_refs.author: Ivan A. Canay, Andres Santos, Azeem M. Shaikh

source_refs.excerpt: residual-tolerance width has the one-sided expansion
strengthen Conjecture 2 from a finite diagnostic taxonomy into a generic-class obstruction or theorem

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature separates sufficient completeness/rank assumptions, nonunique-bridge target identification, and no-completeness partial identification, but it does not give the exact boundary at which the ATE functional remains invariant over the bridge null space. The prior eid_proximal_phase run tried a scalar singular-value threshold but was downgraded because the accepted content was only a finite diagnostic taxonomy; reviewers asked for a generic-class theorem or obstruction, not another LP restatement.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem for a conditional-expectation operator T and ATE functional L, stating point identification iff L annihilates the observationally invisible bridge directions ker(T), with finite-rank corollaries, boundary counterexamples, and an observable sharp-set or residual-width modulus when the condition fails.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the sharp proxy-richness or bridge-compatibility threshold for long-term ATE identification when short-term outcomes act as noisy negative-control proxies for persistent unmeasured confounding.

source_refs.claim: Long-term effects are identified by combining short-term experimental and long-term observational data under persistent confounding, but through sufficient sequential/proxy structure rather than a sharp proxy-richness frontier.
source_refs.author: Guido Imbens, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Yuhao Wang
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.claim: Multiple short-term proxies can identify long-term treatment effects under surrogacy, and the paper studies bias from surrogacy violations rather than a proximal completeness threshold.
source_refs.author: Susan Athey, Raj Chetty, Guido Imbens, Hyunseung Kang

source_refs.claim: Longitudinal proximal inference handles complex time-varying confounding through bridge functions, while bridge-equation solution and regularization remain major technical bottlenecks.
source_refs.author: Andrew Ying, Wang Miao, Xu Shi, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.excerpt: Sharp partial identification of long-term ATE when the surrogate-index assumption fails
minimal K=2,J=1 noisy-panel theorem showing when the earlier wave strictly tightens
common-kernel bridge compatibility statement coupling the experimental measurement kernel to P^B

open_problems.why_unsolved: Long-term causal-inference papers use short-term outcomes, surrogate indices, or sequential proxy structure to overcome persistent confounding, but their assumptions are not expressed as a sharp threshold for when early proxy waves span the confounding direction relevant to the terminal outcome. The prior long-term-surrogate run failed because prevalence dilation and generic LP containment did not prove the reviewer-requested common-kernel bridge compatibility theorem or a minimal noisy-panel strict-tightening result.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite or operator theorem for two-sample long-term ATE stating the exact rank, kernel, or bridge-compatibility condition under which early proxy panels identify the terminal ATE, plus sharp bounds or a counterexample family when the relevant proxy span falls short.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when finite proximal OR/DR residual-squared bridge selectors and minimax negative-control bridge estimators select the same ATE functional at the completeness boundary.

source_refs.claim: Minimax learning targets bridge estimation while relaxing uniqueness and completeness reliance, but does not provide a finite observable equivalence threshold against OR/DR residual selectors.
source_refs.author: Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Masatoshi Uehara

source_refs.claim: Proximal causal learning is cast as a Fredholm inverse problem with kernel moment methods, leaving open the exact relation between different bridge-selection principles near nonidentification.
source_refs.author: Afsaneh Mastouri, Yuchen Zhu, Limor Gultchin, Anna Korba, Ricardo Silva, Matt J. Kusner, Arthur Gretton, Krikamol Muandet

source_refs.claim: Proximal double robustness and efficiency are developed under regularity conditions, but the paper does not give a finite selector-equivalence frontier when bridge equations are weak.
source_refs.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: tau_native(P)=0 is an iff frontier for native population equivalence
real novelty is split across Conjecture penalty-separation and Conjecture selection-range
do not give an observable finite OR/DR-vs-minimax native-equivalence threshold

open_problems.why_unsolved: The estimator literature gives bridge-learning principles, regularization, and proximal DR moments, but not an observable finite criterion telling when two plausible bridge selectors agree on the target despite weak or nonunique bridge equations. The prior eid_proximal_phase review explicitly judged the finite OR/DR-versus-minimax threshold as novel but unproved, with the generic selector-range theorem still missing.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite LP, QP, or KKT theorem proving tau_native(P)=0 iff OR/DR residual selectors and minimax bridge selectors induce the same ATE and first-order object, together with a positive-threshold separation theorem when they differ.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a finite bridge-envelope realization theorem: determine when every bounded observable proximal outcome-bridge solution is induced by an actual latent negative-control causal model.

source_refs.excerpt: every rational h in H(P) is realized by a finite latent proximal model
Conjecture realization is the main sharpness risk
Sharp bridge realisability already assumes the hard endpoint attainability part

open_problems.why_unsolved: Prior proximal and mediation-proxy proposals repeatedly treated observable bridge-fiber extrema as if they were automatically causally realizable. Reviewers flagged that this hides the actual sharpness step: an observed bridge array satisfying linear bridge equations may not correspond to a latent response-type distribution obeying the negative-control exclusions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A Balke-Pearl-style finite response-type construction proving sharp realization for bounded proximal bridge arrays, or a counterexample theorem identifying observable bridge solutions that cannot be completed to any latent negative-control causal law.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop local-to-incomplete weak-proxy inference for proximal ATE estimators when the bridge operator's smallest relevant singular values shrink with sample size.

source_refs.claim: Nonunique bridge solutions can still identify counterfactual means, but the paper does not develop weak-completeness asymptotics as the operator approaches nonidentification.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Weak instruments in NPIV are linked to restricted completeness failure, providing an inverse-problem analogue but not a proximal negative-control inference theory.
source_refs.author: Joachim Freyberger

source_refs.claim: Spectral regularization for econometric inverse problems makes singular-value decay central to estimation difficulty, but does not handle proximal ATE bridge functionals.
source_refs.author: Marine Carrasco, Jean-Pierre Florens, Eric Renault

source_refs.claim: Minimax rates for ill-posed inverse problems separate mildly and severely ill-posed regimes, suggesting a rate-theoretic language for proximal weak-completeness boundaries.
source_refs.author: Xiaohong Chen, Markus Reiss

open_problems.why_unsolved: Proximal semiparametric theory gives root-n inference under regularity, and partial-ID work describes the failure regime after completeness is gone. What is missing is a weak-identification analogue that treats proxy relevance as local to zero, derives the nonstandard limit behavior of bridge-based ATE estimators, and yields confidence sets that remain valid across strong, weak, and failed completeness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local asymptotic experiment for operators T_n with singular values sigma_j,n tending to zero, a phase diagram in n^(1/2) sigma_j,n, weak-limit laws for proximal OR/DR estimators, and uniformly valid Anderson-Rubin-style or projection confidence sets for the ATE functional.

literature_map:
1. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018) anchor negative-control/proxy identification through outcome bridge functions and rank or completeness conditions.
2. Tchetgen Tchetgen, Ying, Cui, Shi, and Miao (2024) present proximal causal inference as proxy-based learning in an inverse-problem setting.
3. Cui, Pu, Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024) give semiparametric efficiency and proximal double robustness, while leaving minimax and weak-proxy boundaries open.
4. Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024) show target means can remain identified without unique bridge functions, sharpening the distinction between bridge uniqueness and target identification.
5. Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023) move from completeness failure to proxy-based partial identification and nonsmooth bounds.
6. Canay, Santos, and Shaikh (2013) warn that completeness can be untestable, so a useful theory should not rely on empirical pretesting of completeness.
7. Carrasco, Florens, and Renault (2007), Chen and Reiss (2011), and Freyberger (2015) provide the inverse-problem and weak-completeness background for singular-value phase behavior.
8. Kallus, Mao, and Uehara (2021), Mastouri et al. (2021), and related proximal-learning papers study minimax or kernel bridge estimation but not finite selector-equivalence thresholds.
9. Ying, Miao, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023) extend proximal methods to longitudinal regimes, where bridge equations become harder and regularization is central.
10. Athey, Chetty, Imbens, and Kang (2019) and Imbens, Kallus, Mao, and Wang (2025) motivate long-term ATE identification with short-term proxies under surrogacy or persistent confounding.
11. The main tension is exact completeness point ID versus no-completeness partial ID; the missing object is a sharp threshold indexed by null spaces, singular values, and target-functional alignment.
12. The supplied study-pipeline gaps were mainly LATE, Manski bounds, and interval-regression inference questions; none survived topic-specific filtering for proximal completeness and long-term ATE.
prior_proposal_map:
Scanned current research/eid_proximal_completeness: only init state/pipeline, no active proposal yet.
Highest yield: _bank/downgraded/eid_proximal_phase_v1 final proposal, rejected angle0, README, and reviews.
Yield from eid_proximal_phase: proximal completeness failure, finite bridge realization, residual modulus, OR/DR-vs-minimax native frontier, selector range, and a binary witness.
Reviewer JSONs for eid_proximal_phase supplied strengthening_paths asking for generic-class proximal obstruction/theorem rather than finite taxonomy.
Second yield: _bank/failed/pid_mediation_proxy_v1 proposal/rejected angle/reviews; useful warnings on bridge realisability and row-span null collapse.
Long-term yield: _bank/failed/pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 proposal/reviews; relevant to long-term ATE and bridge/drift compatibility, but less directly proximal.
Bank README pass found eid_proximal_phase as the only direct proximal-completeness banked item and pid_long_term_surrogate as the closest long-term ATE item.
Pipeline JSONLs were mostly useful only to confirm current qid has no prior proposal and banked items failed/downgraded.
